Decided to sell.prevention: Frame STL as the fallback plan while property is on market. 'If you don't get your price, you'll have £X in the bank by then.'

want to try and self manage.prevention: For GL-SELF leads: Acknowledge the impulse, then ask: 'What does handling two changeovers in the same week look like for you?' Paint the operational reality before presenting the management alternative.

Lead stopped responding.prevention: Send a clean break message at 21 days: 'I'm going to stop chasing and leave the door open — you know where we are.' Paradoxically generates replies.

Gone with another company.prevention: Ask directly in the meeting: 'Are you speaking to anyone else?' If yes: 'What would they need to do differently for you to choose them?' Use the answer to differentiate Stayful before the decision is made.

Setup costs too high.prevention: Present setup cost as a one-time investment against 20+ years of income. 'A £5k setup on a property earning £15k/year = 4 months payback.' For tight-margin properties, present a phased furnishing plan.

Income prediction too low.prevention: R2R leads: qualify income viability before meeting. Own-property leads: check if their expectations are realistic before presenting — anchor them to market data first.

Decided to long let property.prevention: Calculate and show the LTL comparison explicitly. 'You're choosing £X/year over £Y/year — that's £Z less per year you're giving up for predictability.' Some will prefer predictability — qualify for it rather than fight it.

does not fit with Stayful.prevention: Qualify fit earlier. R2R, HMO, overseas-managed properties, leads wanting frequent personal access > 48h need early fit assessment before a full sales meeting.

Input incorrect information.prevention: Verify property existence and lead identity at the pre-screening stage. Check address on Rightmove/Zoopla before booking meeting.

None.prevention: No reason given. Implement a post-abandonment SMS: 'Just curious — what made you decide not to go ahead? No pressure, just helps us improve.'

cross_reference.description: Rent-to-rent leads consistently abandon when income prediction is shown — margins don't work after Stayful fee on top of their landlord rent obligation
cross_reference.prevention: Qualify R2R leads early: ask 'What are you paying the landlord monthly?' before presenting projections. If net STL - (landlord rent + Stayful fee) < £200/month, qualify out.

cross_reference.pattern: SELL profile chose sale over STL
cross_reference.description: Leads who are actively selling often wait to see if they get a good offer — STL is fallback, not primary plan
cross_reference.prevention: Frame STL as insurance: 'If the property doesn't sell in 3 months, it will have earned £X for you.' Reduce commitment friction — make STL feel reversible.

cross_reference.description: Multiple leads cite excessive automated emails as the trigger for disengagement
cross_reference.prevention: Cap automated follow-up at 3 touchpoints. Personal note after 3rd: 'I'll leave you to it — just reply whenever you're ready.' Lower communication volume = higher late-stage conversion.

cross_reference.pattern: Short offer window (< 14 days) on uncertain leads
cross_reference.description: When offer expiry is set before lead has had time to absorb the model, they disengage rather than rush
cross_reference.prevention: Set offer expiry at minimum 14 days. For ABROAD/PURCH leads with genuine delays, extend to 60-90 days with a check-in call at the midpoint.
